title: malus angustifolia var. spinosa
intro: Malus angustifolia var. spinosa, also known as the Dwarf Crabapple or Southern Crabapple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Malus angustifolia var. spinosa include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular natural remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves, bark, or flowers in hot water or a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerin.
